{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 17-year-old girl with severe acne vulgaris that has not responded to standard treatments. Combined oral contraceptive pills (OCs) are prescribed as long-term therapy. The question asks which condition this medication decreases the risk of developing.

From the retrieved documents, specifically Document [5] (Title: Oral Contraceptive Pills -- Indications), it states: 'Strong epidemiologic evidence supports a 50% reduction in the risk of endometrial cancer among women who have used COCs compared with those who have never used COCs. This effect lasts for up to 20 years. COC use decreases the risk of ovarian cancer by 27%; the longer the duration of use, the greater the risk reduction. OCs have also been reported to reduce the risk of colon cancer by 18%.'

This document directly addresses the risk reduction associated with combined oral contraceptive pills. It explicitly mentions a 27% reduction in the risk of ovarian cancer.

The other options—hypertension, cervical cancer, and breast cancer—are not mentioned in the provided documents as conditions reduced by combined oral contraceptives.

Therefore, the correct answer is ovarian cancer.", "answer_choice": "B"}
